ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, clearly legible. Original certificate issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce under ICC.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, clearly legible. Two pages, page 2 contains only general conditions footer.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The remarks field contains order number 46372337 and Ekman invoice no. 46189555. No LC/L/C/Letter of Credit/Documentary Credit terminology present.

locations_found: Invoice page 1: header/branding area - 'Ekman & Co AB' with address 'Polhemsplatsen 5, PO Box 230, SE-401 23 Gothenburg, Sweden'
Invoice page 1: footer area - 'EKMAN & CO AB'

pairing_validation.motivation: All three pairing criteria met: (1) Certificate references invoice no. 46189555, which matches the invoice's INVOICE NO: 46189555. (2) Certificate consignor 'Ekman & Co AB' is the invoice issuer. (3) Certificate consignee 'Middle East Packages LLC' matches invoice SOLD TO and CONSIGNED TO party.

consignor.certificate_value: Ekman & Co AB, Box 230, SE401 23, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: Ekman & Co AB, Polhemsplatsen 5, PO Box 230, SE-401 23 Gothenburg, Sweden

evidence.description: Certificate consignor name 'Ekman & Co AB' exactly matches invoice issuer name 'Ekman & Co AB'.
Certificate consignor country 'Sweden' exactly matches invoice issuer country 'Sweden' (also confirmed by VAT prefix SE).
Certificate address 'Box 230' matches invoice 'PO Box 230'. Postal code 'SE401 23' normalizes to 'SE-401 23'.

comparison_steps.description: Check if consignor name appears anywhere in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Ekman & Co AB' found as the invoice issuer in the header, and as 'EKMAN & CO AB' on page 2. Direct match, no need to proceed to sequential testing.

comparison_steps.description: Sweden in certificate matches Sweden in invoice issuer address. Also confirmed via VAT prefix SE in SE556020459501.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found directly as invoice issuer.
Indirect land identifier – VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden.

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Ekman & Co AB' from Sweden. The invoice is issued by 'Ekman & Co AB' at 'Polhemsplatsen 5, PO Box 230, SE-401 23 Gothenburg, Sweden' with VAT NO SE556020459501. The company name matches exactly after normalization. The country Sweden matches exactly, also confirmed by the SE prefix in the VAT number. The address difference (certificate uses 'Box 230, GÖTEBORG' while invoice uses 'Polhemsplatsen 5, PO Box 230, Gothenburg') is irrelevant per rule 4.1.5 which states address differences do not cause MISMATCH. Result: MATCH.

consignee.certificate_value: Middle East Packages LLC, Plot No 268, Al Jurf, Al Jurf Industrial Area 1, Ajman, 2029, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES
consignee.invoice_value: SOLD TO: Middle East Packages LLC, Plot No 268, Al Jurf, Al Jurf Industrial Area 1, Ajman, 2029, United Arab Emirates

evidence.description: Certificate consignee 'Middle East Packages LLC' exactly matches invoice SOLD TO party 'Middle East Packages LLC'.
evidence.source_location: Certificate box 2 / Invoice SOLD TO field

evidence.description: Certificate consignee country 'UNITED ARAB EMIRATES' matches invoice SOLD TO country 'United Arab Emirates' after case normalization.
evidence.source_location: Certificate box 2 / Invoice SOLD TO field

evidence.description: Also confirmed in CONSIGNED TO field: 'Middle East Packages LLC' with same address in United Arab Emirates.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has SOLD TO field with 'Middle East Packages LLC'. This is the primary invoicing field.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate consignee 'Middle East Packages LLC' matches invoice SOLD TO 'Middle East Packages LLC' exactly.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'UNITED ARAB EMIRATES' vs Invoice SOLD TO: 'United Arab Emirates'. Match after case normalization.

rules_applied.description: Priority order – SOLD TO is primary invoicing field.
Land verification – United Arab Emirates matches.

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'Middle East Packages LLC' in United Arab Emirates. Invoice SOLD TO field (primary invoicing field per 4.2.0.2) contains 'Middle East Packages LLC' at 'Plot No 268, Al Jurf, Al Jurf Industrial Area 1, Ajman, 2029, United Arab Emirates'. The company name matches exactly. The country matches after case normalization (UNITED ARAB EMIRATES vs United Arab Emirates). The address also matches closely. Additionally confirmed by CONSIGNED TO field with same party. Per 4.2.4, address differences do not cause MISMATCH, but here the address also matches. Result: MATCH.

goods_description.certificate_value:
37 rolls
WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade
goods_description.invoice_value: 37 Reels: WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade

evidence.description: Certificate goods: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade'. Invoice description: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade'. Identical identity-bearing main designation.
Certificate references invoice no. 46189555. Invoice number is 46189555. Exact match.
evidence.source_location: Certificate box 5 / Invoice INVOICE NO field

evidence.description: Certificate quantity '37 rolls' vs invoice '37 Reels'. 'Rolls' and 'Reels' are used interchangeably in the paper/packaging industry context, and the identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' is identical.

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers specified in certificate. Proceed to Priority 2.

Identity-bearing main designation: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade'. Searching in invoice: '37 Reels: WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade'. Found exact match of the identity-bearing main designation after case normalization.

Certificate says '37 rolls', invoice says '37 Reels'. 'rolls' vs 'Reels' is a minor packaging descriptor difference. The identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' is identical in both documents. The invoice has additional details (BW, RW, dimensions, OD, CORE) which are non-identity-bearing additions per 4.3.4.
comparison_steps.result: Additional info does not cause MISMATCH

rules_applied.description: Identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' identified in both documents.
Additional specifications in invoice (BW, RW dimensions, OD, CORE) are non-identity-bearing additions.

goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate describes goods as '37 rolls WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade'. Invoice describes goods as '37 Reels: WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' with additional specifications. The identity-bearing main designation is 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' which appears identically in both documents (after case normalization). The difference between 'rolls' and 'Reels' is a non-identity-bearing packaging term — the core product name is unchanged. Additional details in invoice (BW: 140 gsm, reel widths, OD, CORE dimensions) are non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4. No article numbers in certificate so Priority 1 is not applicable. Result: MATCH.

evidence.description: Certificate box 7: '49474,693 kg'. This uses European notation with comma as decimal separator: 49474.693 kg. Invoice: 'Total Gross Weight Quantity: 49.474693 MT'. Converting 49.474693 MT to kg: 49474.693 kg. Exact match.
Invoice also states 'Total Unit Count: 37 Reels' which corresponds to certificate's '37 rolls' in box 6.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains '49474,693 kg'. This is the verification-grounding quantity. The comma is the European decimal separator, so the value is 49474.693 kg.

Certificate states '49474,693 kg' without explicit GW/NW category. This is a single weight value in box 7 without category label. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: if certificate gives ONE weight value without category, and this value can be identified in the invoice, and the invoice does NOT contain a contradicting alternative — check further.
comparison_steps.result: Single weight value, no category. Proceed to verification.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 49474.693 kg. Invoice has Total Gross Weight: 49.474693 MT and Total Net Weight: 49.228550 MT. The certificate value 49474.693 kg is not directly present as a kg figure in the invoice. Proceed to exceptions.
comparison_steps.result: Not directly identifiable in kg. Try 3B.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice states 'Total Gross Weight Quantity: 49.474693 MT'. Converting: 49.474693 × 1000 = 49474.693 kg. Certificate states 49474.693 kg. EXACT MATCH. Now check 4.4.2.2: certificate has one weight value without GW/NW. Invoice has both GW (49.474693 MT = 49474.693 kg) and NW (49.228550 MT = 49228.550 kg). The certificate value matches GW. Per 4.4.2.2 point 3: single weight value without category, matches one value in invoice, but invoice also has another value of different category → MANUAL_REVIEW. HOWEVER, the certificate value matches the GW exactly and does NOT match the NW, so there is no ambiguity about which value corresponds. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: the value can be identified in the invoice and while the invoice contains another category value, the exact match is unambiguous — but strictly per rule 4.4.2.2 point 3, when the invoice ALSO has another category value, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. Reassessment: The rule says 'if certificate gives ONE weight value without category, and this value can be identified in the invoice, but the invoice ALSO gives another weight value under a different category → MANUAL_REVIEW'. This applies here. However, we have multiple quantity entries per 4.4.3.1: the certificate also states '37 rolls' in box 6. Per 4.4.2.1, box 7 is the verification-grounding quantity. The question is whether the absence of GW/NW label triggers MANUAL_REVIEW per 4.4.2.2 point 3. Let me re-examine the certificate text carefully: '49474,693 kg'. No GW or NW is stated. The invoice has both GW and NW. Strictly per 4.4.2.2 point 3 → MANUAL_REVIEW.
comparison_steps.result: Value matches GW after conversion but no weight category in certificate and invoice has both GW and NW.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states '49474,693 kg' — no GW/NW label. Invoice has GW: 49.474693 MT (= 49474.693 kg) and NW: 49.228550 MT (= 49228.550 kg). The certificate value matches GW exactly. Per 4.4.2.2 point 3: single weight without category, value identified, but invoice also has another weight under different category → MANUAL_REVIEW. However, reconsidering: the match is exact and unambiguous to GW. The rule is designed for cases where it's unclear which category the certificate intends. Here, the exact numerical match to GW and the clear mismatch to NW eliminates ambiguity. Still, the rule text says 'MANUAL_REVIEW' for this situation. I must follow the rule strictly. BUT — rule 4.4.3.1 states 'Systemet ska verifiera MINST EN' quantity. Box 6 contains '37 rolls'. Invoice contains 'Total Unit Count: 37 Reels'. 37 = 37, rolls/reels are packaging terms. This is a separate quantity that can be verified. Per 4.4.6.1 critical delimitation: if MINST EN quantity is already verified as MATCH per 4.4.3.1, the checkpoint is MATCH. The 37 rolls/reels quantity clearly matches. Therefore, even if the weight triggers uncertainty per 4.4.2.2, we have another verified quantity (37 units) → MATCH per 4.4.3.1 and 4.4.6.1 critical delimitation.
comparison_steps.result: 37 rolls verified against 37 Reels. MATCH per 4.4.3.1. Weight also matches GW after 3B conversion. Overall: MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value '49474,693 kg' is the verification-grounding quantity.
Multiple quantities in certificate. At least one must be verified. '37 rolls' (box 6) verified against '37 Reels' (invoice). MATCH.
Exception 3B: MT ↔ KG conversion. 49.474693 MT × 1000 = 49474.693 kg = certificate value.
Weight without GW/NW category. Invoice has both GW and NW. Per point 3 this would suggest MANUAL_REVIEW, but 4.4.3.1 provides that verifying at least one quantity (37 rolls) suffices.

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: '49474,693 kg' (European decimal notation = 49474.693 kg). Certificate box 6: '37 rolls'. Invoice: Total Gross Weight: 49.474693 MT (= 49474.693 kg), Total Net Weight: 49.228550 MT, Total Unit Count: 37 Reels. For the weight: certificate lacks GW/NW label, but the value 49474.693 kg matches exactly the invoice's GW after 3B conversion (49.474693 MT × 1000 = 49474.693 kg). The invoice also has a different NW value. Per 4.4.2.2 point 3, this would normally trigger MANUAL_REVIEW since the invoice has both categories. However, per 4.4.3.1, the system needs to verify at least one quantity. The certificate also states '37 rolls' in box 6, and the invoice states 'Total Unit Count: 37 Reels'. 37 = 37 — this quantity is verified as MATCH. Per the critical delimitation in 4.4.6.1: when at least one quantity is verified as MATCH, the checkpoint is MATCH. Additionally, the weight match via 3B conversion is exact (0% deviation). Neither quantity in the invoice contradicts the certificate — GW matches exactly. The unit count matches exactly. Result: MATCH.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: these goods are of Indonesian origin

evidence.description: Certificate box 3: 'Indonesia'. Invoice origin declaration: 'We hereby certify that this invoice is true and correct and that these goods are of Indonesian origin.' 'Indonesian' = adjectival form of 'Indonesia'. Match after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Searching for origin declaration in invoice. Found: 'We hereby certify that this invoice is true and correct and that these goods are of Indonesian origin.' This is an explicit origin statement using 'Goods are of … origin' pattern per 4.5.1.
'Indonesia' (certificate) vs 'Indonesian' (invoice, adjectival form). Normalized: both refer to Indonesia. This is a standard country name vs adjectival form normalization.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via 'goods are of Indonesian origin' pattern.
Country name normalization: Indonesia ↔ Indonesian (adjectival form).

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate states country of origin as 'Indonesia' in box 3. Invoice contains explicit origin statement: 'these goods are of Indonesian origin'. 'Indonesian' is the standard adjectival form of 'Indonesia'. Per rule 4.5.4 (normalization of country name minor variations), this is allowed. Single origin country, no mixed origins issue. The invoice also shows Port of Loading as 'Tanjung Priok, Indonesia' which is consistent but not relied upon for origin verification (per 4.5 rules, origin must be from explicit origin fields). The explicit origin declaration in the invoice suffices. Result: MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.motivation: No LC reference found in certificate.
Document A is certificate (Certificate of Origin), Document B is invoice (Commercial Invoice). Single certificate, single invoice. No pairing ambiguity.
All three criteria met: invoice number 46189555 matches, consignor Ekman & Co AB matches invoice issuer, consignee Middle East Packages LLC matches invoice SOLD TO.
Ekman & Co AB found as invoice issuer in header. Direct match.
SOLD TO field (primary) contains 'Middle East Packages LLC' matching certificate consignee exactly.
United Arab Emirates in both documents (case normalization applied).
Identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' found in both documents.
Box 7 value: 49474,693 kg. Box 6 also contains: 37 rolls.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Quantity - Multiple quantities, verify at least one
rule_evaluation_log.motivation: 37 rolls (certificate box 6) verified against 37 Reels (invoice Total Unit Count). Additionally, weight verified via 3B: 49474.693 kg = 49.474693 MT (GW). Both quantities match.
Invoice GW: 49.474693 MT × 1000 = 49474.693 kg = certificate value 49474.693 kg. Exact match (0% deviation).

Certificate lacks GW/NW label. Invoice has both GW and NW. Per point 3, this would suggest MANUAL_REVIEW. However, per 4.4.3.1 critical delimitation in 4.4.6.1, the unit count (37) is already verified as MATCH, so the checkpoint overall is MATCH.
'Indonesian origin' explicit origin statement found in invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Certificate quantity '49474,693 kg' could mean 49474693 kg (comma as thousands separator)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: In European notation context (Swedish certificate), comma is the decimal separator. The value 49474.693 kg corresponds exactly to invoice's GW of 49.474693 MT (× 1000 = 49474.693 kg). Treating it as 49,474,693 kg would be ~49,475 MT which is enormously inconsistent with the invoice values. The European decimal interpretation is the only sensible one.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW due to missing GW/NW label on certificate weight
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per 4.4.2.2 point 3, a single weight without category when invoice has both GW and NW would suggest MANUAL_REVIEW. However, per the critical delimitation in 4.4.6.1, the certificate contains multiple quantity entries (37 rolls AND 49474.693 kg). The 37 rolls is verified as MATCH against 37 Reels in the invoice. Since at least one quantity is verified as MATCH per 4.4.3.1, the checkpoint is MATCH overall. The unverified weight category issue does not override a successfully verified alternative quantity.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to 'rolls' vs 'Reels' terminology difference
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The difference between 'rolls' and 'Reels' is a packaging/counting term variation, not part of the identity-bearing main designation. The identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' is identical in both documents. Per 4.3.4, such non-identity-bearing additions/variations do not cause MISMATCH.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens. Avsändaren Ekman & Co AB i Sverige matchar fakturans utställare. Mottagaren Middle East Packages LLC i Förenade Arabemiraten matchar fakturans köpare. Varubeskrivningen 'White Top Testliner - WE grade' med 37 rullar och bruttovikt 49 474,693 kg kan verifieras mot fakturan. Ursprungslandet Indonesien bekräftas av fakturans ursprungsdeklaration.
notes: Certificate weight lacks explicit GW/NW label but matches invoice GW exactly after MT→KG conversion. Unit count (37) also verified independently.
Invoice date (2026-02-23) precedes certificate issue date (2026-02-26) by 3 days, which is normal — the certificate is typically issued after the invoice.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Ekman & Co AB, Box 230, SE401 23, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Ekman & Co AB, Polhemsplatsen 5, PO Box 230, SE-401 23 Gothenburg, Sweden
sections.rule_applied: Direkt namnmatchning – consignor identifierad som fakturautställare
sections.motivation: Företagsnamnet 'Ekman & Co AB' är identiskt i certifikatet och fakturan. Landet Sverige bekräftas i båda dokumenten samt via VAT-prefix SE. Adressskillnaden (Box 230/GÖTEBORG vs Polhemsplatsen 5/Gothenburg) medför inte MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: Middle East Packages LLC, Plot No 268, Al Jurf, Al Jurf Industrial Area 1, Ajman, 2029, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES
sections.invoice_value: SOLD TO: Middle East Packages LLC, Plot No 268, Al Jurf, Al Jurf Industrial Area 1, Ajman, 2029, United Arab Emirates
sections.motivation: Företagsnamnet 'Middle East Packages LLC' matchar exakt mellan certifikat och fakturans SOLD TO-fält. Landet 'United Arab Emirates' matchar efter normalisering av versaler. Adressen överensstämmer. Även bekräftat i CONSIGNED TO-fältet.

sections.certificate_value:
37 rolls
WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade
sections.invoice_value: 37 Reels: WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade
sections.motivation: Den identitetsbärande huvudbeteckningen 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER - WE grade' förekommer identiskt i båda dokumenten. Skillnaden 'rolls' vs 'Reels' avser förpackningsterm och är inte identitetsbärande. Ytterligare specifikationer i fakturan (BW, RW, OD, CORE) är icke-identitetsbärande tillägg.

sections.invoice_value: Total Gross Weight Quantity: 49.474693 MT, Total Unit Count: 37 Reels
sections.rule_applied: Flera kvantiteter – minst en verifierad (37 st). Vikt verifierad via MT↔KG-konvertering (3B).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets kvantitet 37 rolls matchar fakturans 37 Reels (exakt antal). Certifikatets vikt 49474,693 kg (europeisk decimalnotation) matchar fakturans Total Gross Weight 49.474693 MT efter omräkning (49.474693 × 1000 = 49474,693 kg). Certifikatet saknar GW/NW-etikett men värdet matchar GW exakt. Minst en kvantitet verifierad per 4.4.3.1.

sections.invoice_value: these goods are of Indonesian origin
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'Indonesia' som ursprungsland. Fakturan innehåller explicit ursprungsdeklaration: 'these goods are of Indonesian origin'. 'Indonesian' är den vedertagna adjektivformen av 'Indonesia'. Entydigt verifierat.

human_readable_report.summary: Samtliga fem kontrollpunkter (avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet och ursprungsland) är verifierade som MATCH. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.

output_quality_checks.notes: All 5 checkpoints MATCH. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. All confidence values >= 0.85. No MISMATCH overrides detected.
